options.name: -AliasesToExport
options.description: Specifies the aliases that the module exports. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -Author
options.description: Specifies the module author.

options.name: -ClrVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum version of the Common Language Runtime (CLR) of the Microsoft .NET Framework that the module requires.

options.name: -CmdletsToExport
options.description: Specifies the cmdlets that the module exports. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -CompanyName
options.description: Identifies the company or vendor who created the module.

options.name: -CompatiblePSEditions
options.description: Specifies the module's compatible PSEditions. For information about PSEdition, see Modules with compatible PowerShell Editions (/powershell/gallery/concepts/module-psedition-support).

options.name: -Copyright
options.description: Specifies a copyright statement for the module.

options.name: -DefaultCommandPrefix
options.description: Specifies a prefix that is prepended to the nouns of all commands in the module when they're imported into a session. Enter a prefix string. Prefixes prevent command name conflicts in a user's session.

options.name: -Description
options.description: Describes the contents of the module.

options.name: -DotNetFrameworkVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum version of the Microsoft .NET Framework that the module requires.

options.name: -DscResourcesToExport
options.description: Specifies the Desired State Configuration (DSC) resources that the module exports. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -ExternalModuleDependencies
options.description: A list of external modules that this module is depends on.

options.name: -FileList
options.description: Specifies all items that are included in the module.

options.name: -FormatsToProcess
options.description: Specifies the formatting files (`.ps1xml`) that run when the module is imported.

options.name: -FunctionsToExport
options.description: Specifies the functions that the module exports. Wildcards are permitted.

options.name: -Guid

args.name: System.Guid
args.default: A GUID generated for the module

options.description: Specifies a unique identifier for the module. The GUID can be used to distinguish among modules with the same name.

options.name: -HelpInfoUri
options.description: Specifies the internet address of the HelpInfo XML file for the module. Enter a Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) that begins with http or https .

options.name: -IconUri
options.description: Specifies the URL of an icon for the module. The specified icon is displayed on the gallery web page for the module.

options.name: -LicenseUri
options.description: Specifies the URL of licensing terms for the module.

options.name: -ModuleList
options.description: Lists all modules that are included in this module.

options.name: -ModuleVersion
options.description: Specifies the module's version.

options.name: -NestedModules
options.description: Specifies script modules (`.psm1`) and binary modules (`.dll`) that are imported into the module's session state. The files in the NestedModules key run in the order in which they're listed in the value.

options.name: -PassThru
options.description: Writes the resulting module manifest to the console and creates a `.psd1` file. By default, this cmdlet doesn't generate any output.

options.name: -Path
options.description: Specifies the path and file name of the new module manifest. Enter a path and file name with a `.psd1` file name extension, such as `$pshome\Modules\MyModule\MyModule.psd1`. The Path parameter is required.

options.name: -PowerShellHostName
options.description: Specifies the name of the PowerShell host program that the module requires. Enter the name of the host program, such as Windows PowerShell ISE Host or ConsoleHost . Wildcards aren't permitted.

options.name: -PowerShellHostVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum version of the PowerShell host program that works with the module. Enter a version number, such as 1.1.

options.name: -PowerShellVersion
options.description: Specifies the minimum version of PowerShell that works with this module. For example, you can enter 1.0, 2.0, or 3.0 as the parameter's value. It must be in an X.X format. For example, if you submit `5`, PowerShell will throw an error.

options.name: -Prerelease
options.description: Prerelease string of this module. Adding a Prerelease string identifies the module as a prerelease version. When the module is published to the PowerShell Gallery, this data is used to identify prerelease packages. To acquire prerelease packages from the Gallery, you must use the AllowPrerelease parameter with the PowerShellGet commands `Find-Module`, `Install-Module`, `Update-Module`, and `Save-Module`.

options.name: -PrivateData
options.description: Specifies data that is passed to the module when it's imported.

options.name: -ProcessorArchitecture
options.description: Specifies the processor architecture that the module requires. Valid values are x86, AMD64, IA64, MSIL, and None (unknown or unspecified).

options.name: -ProjectUri
options.description: Specifies the URL of a web page about this project.

options.name: -ReleaseNotes
options.description: Specifies release notes.

options.name: -RequiredAssemblies
options.description: Specifies the assembly (`.dll`) files that the module requires. Enter the assembly file names. PowerShell loads the specified assemblies before updating types or formats, importing nested modules, or importing the module file that is specified in the value of the RootModule key.

options.name: -RequiredModules
options.description: Specifies modules that must be in the global session state. If the required modules aren't in the global session state, PowerShell imports them. If the required modules aren't available, the `Import-Module` command fails.

options.name: -RequireLicenseAcceptance
options.description: Flag to indicate whether the module requires explicit user acceptance for install, update, or save.

name: -RootModule
-ModuleToProcess
options.description: Specifies the primary or root file of the module. Enter the file name of a script (`.ps1`), a script module (`.psm1`), a module manifest(`.psd1`), an assembly (`.dll`), a cmdlet definition XML file (`.cdxml`), or a workflow (`.xaml`). When the module is imported, the members that are exported from the root module file are imported into the caller's session state.

options.name: -ScriptsToProcess
options.description: Specifies script (`.ps1`) files that run in the caller's session state when the module is imported. You can use these scripts to prepare an environment, just as you might use a login script.

options.name: -Tags
options.description: Specifies an array of tags.

options.name: -TypesToProcess
options.description: Specifies the type files (`.ps1xml`) that run when the module is imported.

options.name: -VariablesToExport
options.description: Specifies the variables that the module exports. Wildcards are permitted.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if `New-ModuleManifest` runs. The cmdlet isn't run.
